text:
There was an old fisherman who lived near this strange island. He tended his bobbing nets each dawn and often watched the clouds, hoping to spot something unusual.

One morning, he came upon a peculiar bird. Its feathers were iridescent green, and its beak looked like a bill filled with tiny pearls. It sang a song so enchanting that even the fish wanted to listen. The bird taught him songs that sounded like laughter echoing over rolling waves. That summer, the fisherman taught the bird how to sing, and it would chirp his name whenever it wanted a visit.

Years passed, and the bird didn't return in seasons. The fisherman was sad every time. One cold winter, right before Christmas, the bird suddenly came back. It perched on the fishermen's shoulder and sang a haunting melody that sent tingles down his spine.

"Come back," he whispered.

The bird sang something he couldn't understand, a language all its own. The next morning,
